qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Code injection via OpenAPI path routes enables arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies route string interpolation through getRoute() and generated options; the advisory root cause is x-enumDescriptions embedded without escaping in getEnumImplementation() during enum generation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in OpenAPI spec URL fetching allows internal network access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unrestricted URL fetching in import-specs/fetchUrls; it does not identify enum generation, x-enumDescriptions handling, or the getEnumImplementation() injection sink described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in output file paths allows arbitrary file write/delete
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded output path resolution and file writes/deletes; it is unrelated to x-enumDescriptions, const enum generation, or getEnumImplementation().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: unsanitized x-enumDescriptions flowing into getEnumImplementation() during const enum generation and becoming executable generated schema code.
evidence: The advisory describes arbitrary code injection via x-enumDescriptions embedded without proper escaping in getEnumImplementation() during const enum generation.

vuln-code-injection-route-path identifies OpenAPI path route interpolation through packages/core/src/getters/route.ts and packages/core/src/generators/options.ts, a different input field and code path.
vuln-ssrf-openapi-spec-fetching identifies unrestricted remote URL fetching, not generated enum description code injection.
vuln-path-traversal-output-path identifies output path traversal in file writes/deletes, not enum generation.
